title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is considered a prime trade candidate, offering versatility and defensive prowess for the Celtics.
LeBron James could be a major free-agent target, providing leadership and playmaking abilities despite being in his 40s.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are other potential targets to address forward depth, center issues, and guard scoring.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advances to NCAA Division II World Series after defeating Molloy University in a best-of-three series.
Endicott College reaches NCAA Division III championship round for the fourth consecutive year by defeating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott will compete in the World Series, locations and opponents for both still to be determined.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn's Alex Karaban next week ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, a Southborough native, helped UConn win two NCAA championships and was named All-Big East First-team.
Karaban ended his UConn career with 1,880 points, ranking sixth all-time in program history.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth blended jazz and pop at MGM Music Hall, focusing on musicianship over spectacle with stripped-down aesthetics.
The tour promotes his March release 'Whatever’s Clever!' and includes hits that sparked high audience engagement.
Opener Daniel Seavey captivated with live looping, while Puth connected with the crowd through humor and nostalgia.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly, marking its 30th anniversary.
Following their album performance, the band covered Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' album in its entirety.
Jakob Dylan's performance highlighted his expressive voice and dynamic stage presence, supported by musicians Aaron Embry and Chris Masterson.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents last year, continuing a post-pandemic trend.
Everett and Worcester experienced the most significant population growth in the state, gaining 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
Massachusetts' overall population grew by 15,500 residents at only 0.2%, with high housing costs and less immigration impacting numbers.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Market Basket's parent ranked on Forbes' largest family businesses list.
Fidelity, led by the Johnson family, ranked 14th with $37.7 billion revenue.
Demoulas Super Markets, fully owned by the Demoulas family, ranked 69th with $8 billion revenue.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a comprehensive campus housing overhaul.
The project will modernize facilities, expand amenities, and maintain affordability amid growing student enrollment.
Redevelopment will occur in phases, allowing students to remain on campus during construction.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford, featuring tracks from her new album 'World's Gone Wrong'.
Williams' setlist included protest songs and covers, reflecting her activism and critique of current political issues.
Due to a stroke in 2020, Williams did not play guitar but focused on vocals with her band backing.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Red Sox lost to the Twins 8-6 at Fenway Park, despite a strong start by Payton Tolle with nine strikeouts.
Justin Slaten, entering in the seventh inning, allowed four hits and four runs, including two home runs, before being pulled.
Boston's offense managed six runs, a rare occurrence this season, yet was overshadowed by Minnesota's larger offensive output.
